message:
chore(beads): add comprehensive beads for toon_rust installer automation

Epic bd-3a6: Automate toon_rust binary installation in UBS installer

Sub-beads with dependency chain:
- bd-3a6.1: Research install.sh patterns (ready)
- bd-3a6.2: Set up GitHub releases with binaries (ready, parallel)
- bd-3a6.3: Add download_toon_binary() function (blocked by .1, .2)
- bd-3a6.4: Integrate into main installer flow (blocked by .3)
- bd-3a6.5: Auto-configure TOON_BIN in ubs (blocked by .4)
- bd-3a6.6: Test on Linux/macOS (blocked by .5)
- bd-3a6.7: Update documentation (blocked by .6)

Also added:
- bd-2mro: Publish toon_rust as library crate to crates.io

All beads are self-documenting with detailed descriptions covering:
- Background and rationale
- Implementation details
- Testing requirements
- Dependencies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use run_manifest.py infrastructure.","notes":"Added TOON format test cases: toon-format-js-buggy, toon-format-env-var, toon-format-fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:53:49.745374942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T06:01:58.015415776Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T06:01:58.015358028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2kp","title":"Implement JSON-to-TOON piping via tr binary","description":"In the ubs meta-runner, when --format=toon is specified, generate the JSON report internally (reusing existing merge_json_scanners logic) then pipe through the tr binary to produce TOON output. Depends on bd-3n7 (flag recognition).","notes":"TOON piping implemented via toon binary with fallback to JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:53:45.611878434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T05:57:51.062467118Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T05:57:51.062388350Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-2mro","title":"Feature: Publish toon_rust as library crate to crates.io","description":"## Overview

Publish toon_rust to crates.io as a library crate with both lib and bin targets, enabling Rust projects to use TOON encoding/decoding without spawning a subprocess.

## Background

Currently toon_rust is only available as a binary (`tr` / `toon-encode`). This works for CLI tools and bash scripts, but Rust projects that want TOON support must:
1. Shell out to the binary (subprocess overhead)
2. Parse stdout/stderr (fragile)
3. Handle binary availability (may not be installed)

A library crate solves all these issues.

## Proposed API

```rust
// lib.rs
pub fn encode(json: &str) -> Result<String, ToonError>;
pub fn encode_value(value: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<String, ToonError>;
pub fn decode(toon: &str) -> Result<String, ToonError>;
pub fn decode_value(toon: &str) -> Result<serde_json::Value, ToonError>;

// With options
pub fn encode_with_options(json: &str, opts: EncodeOptions) -> Result<String, ToonError>;

pub struct EncodeOptions {
    pub delimiter: Delimiter,      // Comma, Tab, Pipe
    pub indent: usize,             // Default 2
    pub key_folding: KeyFolding,   // Off, Safe
    pub flatten_depth: Option<usize>,
}
```

## Cargo.toml Structure

```toml
[package]
name = "toon"
version = "0.2.0"

[lib]
name = "toon"
path = "src/lib.rs"

[[bin]]
name = "toon-encode"
path = "src/main.rs"
```

## Usage Examples

```rust
use toon::{encode, decode};

let json = r#"{"name": "test", "values": [1, 2, 3]}"#;
let toon_output = encode(json)?;
let roundtrip = decode(&toon_output)?;
assert_eq!(json, roundtrip);
```

## Benefits

1. **No subprocess overhead** - Direct function calls
2. **Type safety** - Rust error handling
3. **Composability** - Use in pipelines, iterators
4. **Testability** - Unit test TOON encoding in Rust projects
5. **Discoverability** - Found via crates.io search

## Migration Path

- Existing binary users: No change (bin target still works)
- New Rust projects: `cargo add toon` and use library API
- UBS: Could optionally include as dependency if rewritten in Rust

## Dependencies

None - can be done independently.

## Notes

This work happens in the toon_rust repository.
Consider whether to rename the crate from `toon_rust` to just `toon`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-24T07:01:31.624598652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T07:01:31.624598652Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates-io","feature","library","toon"]}
 {"id":"bd-3a6","title":"Epic: Automate toon_rust binary installation in UBS installer","description":"## Overview

The UBS installer (`install.sh`) must automatically download and install the toon_rust `tr` binary as part of the standard installation process. Users should NOT need to manually:
- Download toon_rust
- Build from source
- Set TOON_BIN environment variable
- Configure shell profiles

Everything must be fully automated, just like ast-grep, ripgrep, and jq installations are today.

## Background & Rationale

### Current State
- UBS supports `--format=toon` for ~50% smaller output (optimized for LLM token efficiency)
- TOON encoding currently uses either:
  - Node.js `toon` CLI (@toon-format/cli) - requires Node.js, slower
  - toon_rust `tr` binary - 10x faster, no runtime dependencies
- User must manually set `TOON_BIN=/path/to/tr` to use the fast Rust version
- This friction means most users get the slow Node.js version by default

### Why This Matters
- toon_rust is ~10x faster than the Node.js implementation
- No Node.js runtime dependency required
- Consistent with UBS philosophy: batteries-included, zero-config
- TOON format is critical for AI/LLM workflows where token efficiency matters

### Naming Conflict Issue
- toon_rust binary is named `tr` which conflicts with Unix `tr` (translate command)
- Solution: Install as `toon-encode` in ~/.local/bin to avoid PATH conflicts
- UBS already sets TOON_BIN, so the binary name is abstracted away

## Success Criteria
1. Fresh UBS install automatically downloads platform-appropriate toon_rust binary
2. Binary installed to ~/.local/bin/toon-encode (or platform equivalent)
3. TOON_BIN automatically configured (shell profile or ubs internal default)
4. `ubs --format=toon` works out-of-box with Rust encoder
5. Graceful fallback to Node.js toon if Rust binary unavailable
6. Works on Linux (x86_64, arm64) and macOS (x86_64, arm64)

## Architecture Notes
- Follow existing patterns from install.sh (see ast-grep, ripgrep, jq downloads)
- Use GitHub releases for binary distribution
- Verify checksums for security
- Support --dry-run for testing","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:51:33.356904216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T06:51:33.356904216Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","epic","installer","toon"]}
 {"id":"bd-3a6.1","title":"Research: Analyze install.sh binary download patterns","description":"## Task

Study the existing binary download infrastructure in install.sh to understand:
1. How `download_binary_release()` function works
2. Platform detection logic (Linux/macOS, x86_64/arm64)
3. Checksum verification patterns
4. Error handling and fallback strategies
5. Installation directory conventions (~/.local/bin)

## Key Functions to Study

- `download_binary_release()` (~line 982)
- `verify_download_checksum()` (~line 526)
- Platform detection in `detect_platform()`
- ast-grep installation logic as reference implementation

## Deliverables

- Document the pattern for adding new binary downloads
- Identify any reusable helper functions
- Note any platform-specific considerations
- List required changes for toon_rust integration

## Files to Examine

- /data/projects/ultimate_bug_scanner/install.sh (lines 982-1150 for binary download)
- GitHub releases structure for existing binaries

## Notes

This is a research task - no code changes. Output should inform subsequent implementation beads.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:52:13.878131012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T06:52:13.878131012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["installer","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:52:13.878131012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3a6.2","title":"Infra: Set up toon_rust GitHub releases with platform binaries","description":"## Task

Configure toon_rust repository to publish pre-built binaries for all supported platforms via GitHub releases.

## Required Release Assets

For each release (e.g., v0.2.0), publish:
- `toon-encode-linux-x86_64.tar.gz` - Linux AMD64
- `toon-encode-linux-aarch64.tar.gz` - Linux ARM64
- `toon-encode-darwin-x86_64.tar.gz` - macOS Intel
- `toon-encode-darwin-aarch64.tar.gz` - macOS Apple Silicon
- `SHA256SUMS` - Checksums for all assets
- `SHA256SUMS.minisig` - Signed checksums (optional but recommended)

## Implementation Options

1. **GitHub Actions workflow** (recommended)
   - Cross-compile on release tag push
   - Use `cross` for Linux targets
   - Native build for macOS via matrix

2. **Manual release process**
   - Document build commands for each platform
   - Less ideal but works

## Binary Naming

The binary inside the tarball should be named `toon-encode` (not `tr`) to:
- Avoid conflict with Unix `tr` command
- Make purpose clear
- Allow safe PATH inclusion

## Tarball Structure

```
toon-encode-linux-x86_64.tar.gz
└── toon-encode  (the binary)
```

## Reference

See how ast-grep does releases:
https://github.com/ast-grep/ast-grep/releases

## Dependencies

None - can be done in parallel with other work.

## Notes

This work happens in the toon_rust repository, not ultimate_bug_scanner.
The UBS installer will consume these release assets.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:53:15.973060188Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T06:53:15.973060188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["infra","releases","toon"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:53:15.973060188Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3a6.3","title":"Core: Add download_toon_binary() function to install.sh","description":"## Task

Implement `download_toon_binary()` function in install.sh following the established pattern from `download_binary_release()`.

## Function Signature

```bash
download_toon_binary() {
  local platform="$1"  # linux or darwin
  local arch="$2"      # x86_64 or aarch64
  # Returns 0 on success, 1 on failure
}
```

## Implementation Details

1. **Construct download URL**
   ```bash
   local version="0.2.0"  # or fetch latest from GitHub API
   local asset="toon-encode-${platform}-${arch}.tar.gz"
   local url="https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/toon_rust/releases/download/v${version}/${asset}"
   ```

2. **Download and verify**
   - Use existing `verify_download_checksum()` pattern
   - Fetch SHA256SUMS from release
   - Verify tarball checksum before extraction

3. **Extract and install**
   - Extract to temp directory
   - Move binary to `$HOME/.local/bin/toon-encode`
   - Set executable permissions (chmod +x)

4. **Error handling**
   - Log failures with context
   - Return non-zero on any failure
   - Don't leave partial installations

## Location in install.sh

Add after `download_binary_release()` function (~line 1150).

## Dependencies

- bd-3a6.1 (Research: understand existing patterns)
- bd-3a6.2 (Infra: release assets must exist)

## Testing

- Test on Linux x86_64
- Test on macOS arm64
- Test with network failures (should fail gracefully)
- Test --dry-run mode","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:54:27.421730901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T06:55:31.858131122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","implementation","installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:54:27.421730901Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:55:25.646133314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:55:31.856718982Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3a6.4","title":"Core: Integrate toon_rust installation into main installer flow","description":"## Task

Add toon_rust binary installation to the main installer workflow, making it part of the standard UBS installation process.

## Integration Point

In install.sh, after other binary installations (ast-grep, jq, etc.), add:

```bash
# Install TOON encoder (toon_rust) for fast --format=toon support
install_toon_encoder() {
  local platform arch
  platform="$(detect_platform)"
  arch="$(detect_arch)"
  
  log "Installing TOON encoder (toon_rust)..."
  
  if download_toon_binary "$platform" "$arch"; then
    success "TOON encoder installed to ~/.local/bin/toon-encode"
    # Update TOON_BIN in ubs script or shell profile
    configure_toon_bin
  else
    warn "TOON encoder installation failed. Falling back to Node.js toon."
    warn "For best performance, install manually: cargo install toon_rust"
  fi
}
```

## Call Site

Add to main installation sequence (~line 1200-1300):
```bash
# After install_dependencies()
install_toon_encoder
```

## Conditional Installation

Consider making this optional with a flag:
- `--no-toon` to skip TOON encoder installation
- Default: install it

## Graceful Degradation

If toon_rust installation fails:
1. Log warning (not error)
2. Continue with installation
3. TOON format will use Node.js fallback (if available)
4. Or fall back to JSON format

## Dependencies

- bd-3a6.3 (Core: download_toon_binary() function must exist)

## Testing

- Full installation on fresh system
- Installation with --no-toon flag
- Installation when download fails (network issues)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:56:22.440041723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T06:56:26.884502038Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","implementation","installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:56:22.440041723Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:56:26.883179968Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3a6.5","title":"Core: Auto-configure TOON_BIN in ubs script","description":"## Task

Modify the ubs script to automatically detect and use the installed toon-encode binary without requiring user configuration.

## Current State

```bash
# In ubs script (line ~147)
TOON_BIN="${TOON_BIN:-toon}"  # Defaults to Node.js toon
```

## Proposed Change

```bash
# Auto-detect toon_rust binary location, fall back to toon (Node.js)
detect_toon_bin() {
  local candidates=(
    "$HOME/.local/bin/toon-encode"     # Standard install location
    "$HOME/.cargo/bin/toon-encode"     # Cargo install location
    "/usr/local/bin/toon-encode"       # System-wide install
  )
  for bin in "${candidates[@]}"; do
    if [[ -x "$bin" ]]; then
      echo "$bin"
      return 0
    fi
  done
  # Fall back to PATH lookup
  if command -v toon-encode >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    command -v toon-encode
    return 0
  fi
  # Final fallback to Node.js toon
  echo "toon"
  return 0
}

TOON_BIN="${TOON_BIN:-$(detect_toon_bin)}"
```

## Why This Approach

1. **Zero user configuration** - Works out of box after installation
2. **Respects user override** - TOON_BIN env var still works
3. **Graceful fallback** - Uses Node.js toon if Rust binary not found
4. **Multiple install locations** - Supports different installation methods

## Location

Modify ubs script near line 147 where TOON_BIN is defined.

## Dependencies

- bd-3a6.4 (Core: integration - binary must be installed first)

## Testing

- Fresh install: should auto-detect ~/.local/bin/toon-encode
- Existing Node.js users: should still work with toon fallback
- Manual override: TOON_BIN=/custom/path should work","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:58:15.463448161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T06:58:19.578793453Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","implementation","ubs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:58:15.463448161Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:58:19.577652414Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3a6.6","title":"Test: Verify toon_rust installation on Linux and macOS","description":"## Task

Create and execute comprehensive tests for toon_rust binary installation across all supported platforms.

## Test Matrix

| Platform | Architecture | Test Environment |
|----------|--------------|------------------|
| Linux | x86_64 | GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest |
| Linux | aarch64 | GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest (qemu) or self-hosted |
| macOS | x86_64 | GitHub Actions macos-13 |
| macOS | aarch64 | GitHub Actions macos-latest (M1) |

## Test Cases

### 1. Fresh Installation
```bash
# Clean environment (no existing ubs)
./install.sh
# Verify: ~/.local/bin/toon-encode exists and is executable
# Verify: ubs --format=toon produces TOON output
```

### 2. Binary Verification
```bash
toon-encode --version  # Should show toon_rust version
echo "{\"test\":1}" | toon-encode  # Should produce TOON output
```

### 3. Integration Test
```bash
ubs --format=toon test-suite/buggy 2>&1 | grep -q "project:"
# Should use toon-encode (check stderr for "Rust" or timing)
```

### 4. Fallback Test
```bash
rm ~/.local/bin/toon-encode
ubs --format=toon test-suite/buggy  # Should fall back to Node.js toon
```

### 5. Performance Comparison
```bash
time ubs --format=toon . > /dev/null  # With toon_rust
TOON_BIN=toon time ubs --format=toon . > /dev/null  # With Node.js
# Rust should be ~10x faster
```

## CI Integration

Add to existing GitHub Actions workflow or create new test-installer.yml:
```yaml
jobs:
  test-install:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, macos-13]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: ./install.sh
      - run: ~/.local/bin/toon-encode --version
      - run: ./ubs --format=toon test-suite/buggy
```

## Dependencies

- bd-3a6.5 (Core: auto-configure - full implementation must be complete)

## Deliverables

- Test script or CI workflow
- Test results for all platforms
- Performance benchmarks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:59:25.748708657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T06:59:28.632124270Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","installer","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:59:25.748708657Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T06:59:28.630463563Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3a6.7","title":"Docs: Update README and help text with TOON installation info","description":"## Task

Update all documentation to reflect the automated TOON encoder installation.

## Files to Update

### 1. README.md

Add to Installation section:
```markdown
### TOON Format Support

UBS automatically installs the high-performance toon_rust encoder for 
`--format=toon` output. This provides ~50% smaller output compared to 
JSON, optimized for LLM token efficiency.

The Rust encoder is ~10x faster than the Node.js alternative and requires
no runtime dependencies.
```

Update Output Formats section:
```markdown
- `--format=toon` - TOON format (~50% smaller, uses toon_rust encoder)
```

### 2. install.sh help text

Update usage message to mention TOON encoder installation.

### 3. ubs --help

Already updated (TOON_BIN documented). Verify accuracy.

### 4. RESEARCH_FINDINGS.md

Update the documentation to reflect:
- Binary is installed as toon-encode (not tr)
- Auto-detection in ubs script
- No manual configuration required

## Key Messages

1. **Zero configuration** - Works out of box
2. **Performance** - 10x faster than Node.js
3. **Fallback** - Gracefully uses Node.js if Rust binary unavailable
4. **Override** - TOON_BIN env var for custom setups

## Dependencies

- bd-3a6.6 (Test: ensure implementation is verified before documenting)

## Notes

Documentation should be updated AFTER testing confirms everything works.
Avoids documenting features that may change during implementation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T07:00:22.689367216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T07:00:26.752347895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","readme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-24T07:00:22.689367216Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3a6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-24T07:00:26.751108141Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3n7","title":"Add --format=toon CLI flag to meta-runner","description":"Add toon as a valid value for the --format flag in the ubs meta-runner script. The flag is parsed around line 369-425. Add toon to the valid values list and add a new case in the output emission switch at line 2407-2452. This is the first step - just recognize the flag, actual implementation comes in ubs-toon-pipe.","notes":"TOON format integration complete: CLI flag, module execution, fallback behavior","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:53:24.636916914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T05:57:45.361584010Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T05:57:45.361526602Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Supports: text, json, jsonl, sarif, toon.","notes":"Added UBS_OUTPUT_FORMAT and TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT env var support with proper precedence","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:53:46.575066692Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T05:59:57.411337722Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T05:59:57.411259184Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9vy","title":"Graceful fallback if tr binary not in PATH","description":"When --format=toon is requested but the tr binary is not found in PATH, emit a warning to stderr and fall back to JSON format. Check with command -v tr or which tr. Print: 'Warning: tr (TOON encoder) not found in PATH; falling back to --format=json'. This prevents hard failures in environments without tr installed.","notes":"Implemented fallback to JSON with warning when toon binary not in PATH","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:53:48.617231557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T05:59:02.191991743Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T05:59:02.191928574Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
